gallery.cap: National Scenic Byway Wetlands and Wildlife (Kansas) NARA 68886364 (pa

gallery.desc: Heading into Quivira from the northwest, this is the typical autumn scene you will encounter. This road takes you on east to the Wildlife Drive on the north end of the refuge. Location: Quivira National Wildlife Refuge, Kansas (38.172° N 98.527° W) Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Barton County Arts Council Oscar Micheaux Display
gallery.desc: A number of posters on a storyboard show the work of Oscar Devereaux Micheaux, author, film director, and independent producer of over 40 films in the early 20th century. Location: Great Bend, Kansas Status: Public domain. Provided by the Great Bend Convention and Visitors Bureau

gallery.cap: Barton County Historical Village Exodusters Display
gallery.desc: A display of historical artifacts begins the Exoduster exhibit at the Barton County Historical Village. Location: Great Bend, Kansas Status: Public domain. Provided by the Great Bend Convention and Visitors Bureau

Surrounded by flowers on a sunny summer day, the Barton Historical Village's Western Union Telegraph and Cable Office teaches visitors about old-fashioned communication. Location: Kansas Status: Public domain. Provided by the Great Bend Convention and Visitors Bureau

A Great Blue Heron attempts to swallow a fair-sized (approximately 3-pound) largemouth bass at Quivira National Wildlife Refuge. Location: Quivira National Wildlife Refuge, Kansas (38.462° N 98.627° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Jerry Segraves

gallery.cap: Birds of Prey Flighted Raptor Show
gallery.desc: A man demonstrates a flighted bird of prey during National Wildlife Refuge Week at Quivira National Wildlife Refuge during October of every year. Location: Kansas (38.447° N 98.620° W) Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Martin Cemetery in St. John, Kansas
gallery.desc: A small sign marks the Martin Cemetery in St. John, Kansas, an African-American cemetery where a number of Exodusters are buried. Location: St. John, Kansas Status: Public domain. Provided by the Great Bend Convention and Visitors Bureau

gallery.cap: Martin Cemetery on a Sunny Day
gallery.desc: The Martin Cemetery, final resting place for a number of former slaves, sits amid green trees on a sunny day. Location: St. John, Kansas Status: Public domain. Provided by the Great Bend Convention and Visitors Bureau

A serene park in St. John, Kansas provides a sunny area to enjoy a peaceful break next to the water fountain. Location: St. John, Kansas Status: Public domain. Provided by the Great Bend Convention and Visitors Bureau

gallery.cap: Samuel Gracey's Grave in the Eden Valley Cemetery
gallery.desc: The grave of Samuel Gracey, Exoduster and Stafford County homesteader, rests peacefully in the Eden Valley Cemetery. Location: Great Bend, Kansas Status: Public domain. Provided by the Great Bend Convention and Visitors Bureau

gallery.cap: Songbird Trail at Cheyenne Bottoms Interpretive Center Area
gallery.desc: This shelter belted songbird area was developed at the site of the Kansas Wetlands Interpretive Center to provide variety to the birdwatcher's "menu". Benches, feeders, and some interpretive signage is provided along this half mile walk. This trail will soon connect to a boardwalk through the adjacent mitigation marsh. The Byway provides many opportunities for those who like to view nature "on foo
A Civil War Cannon sits on display in the Stafford County Museum. Location: Stafford County, Kansas Status: Public domain. Provided by the Great Bend Convention and Visitors Bureau

A storyboard display at the Stafford County Museum explains the role of Exodusters in the history of Stafford County. Location: Stafford County, Kansas Status: Public domain. Provided by the Great Bend Convention and Visitors Bureau

gallery.cap: The Courthouse Soldier at "The Rifleman" Monument
gallery.desc: A statue of a Civil War soldier, known as "The Rifleman" Monument, stands in the Barton County Courthouse Square as a memorial to the lives of local Civil War veterans. Location: Barton County Courthouse Square, Kansas Status: Public domain. Provided by the Great Bend Convention and Visitors Bureau

The Rifleman stands guard over the Barton County Courthouse Square, commemorating the lives of local Civil War veterans. Location: Barton County Courthouse Square, Kansas Status: Public domain. Provided by the Great Bend Convention and Visitors Bureau

The sun glints off the Wetlands and Wildlife Scenic Byway sign in front of the Kansas Wetlands Education Center. Location: Great Bend, Kansas (38.361° N 98.765° W) Status: Public domain. Photo courtesy of Cris Collier.

A group of birders gather at Cheyenne Bottoms Wildlife Area for the Wings and Wetlands Birding Festival. Location: Cheyenne Bottoms Wildlife Area, Kansas (38.447° N 98.660° W) Status: Public domain. Great Bend Convention Visitors Bureau
